title: Madhuca kuchingensis
intro: Madhuca kuchingensis is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae, commonly known as the Kuching stink hill. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases. The bioactive constituents of Madhuca kuchingensis include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Madhuca kuchingensis include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, often used to treat ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections.
